channel.title: AI at Meta Blog
channel.description: AI research, open source, and applications from Meta

item.title: Introducing Muse Spark 1.1
item.description: Introducing Muse Spark 1.1

item.title: Introducing Muse Image and Muse Video
item.description: Introducing Muse Image and Muse Video

item.title: From Brain Waves to Words: Brain2Qwerty Offers a New Path to Communication Without Surgery
item.description: From Brain Waves to Words: Brain2Qwerty Offers a New Path to Communication Without Surgery

item.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
item.description: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence

item.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
item.description: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI

item.title: How Alta Daily Uses Meta’s Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing and Global Reasoning
Introducing TRIBE v2: A Predictive Foundation Model Trained to Understand How the Human Brain Processes Complex Stimuli
Mapping the World's Forests with Greater Precision: Introducing Canopy Height Maps v2
item.description: Today, in partnership with the World Resources Institute, we’re announcing Canopy Height Maps v2 (CHMv2): an open source model and world-scale maps generated with it.

item.title: Reducing Government Costs and Increasing Access to Greenspaces in the United Kingdom with DINO
item.description: Meta's DINOv2 model is enhancing reforestation efforts around the world. Learn how the UK government is using DINO to help reduce costs and increase access to greenspaces.

item.title: The Universities Space Research Association Applies Segment Anything Model for Responding to Flood Emergencies
item.description: The Universities Space Research Association and Meta are collaborating to help support water observing systems set up by the U.S. Geological Survey.

item.title: How DINO and SAM are Helping Modernize Essential Medical Triage Practices
item.description: By leveraging advanced AI models, teams at the University of Pennsylvania are aiming to bring cutting-edge automation to emergency response.

item.title: Introducing SAM Audio: The First Unified Multimodal Model for Audio Separation
item.description: SAM Audio transforms audio processing by making it easy to isolate any sound from complex audio mixtures using natural, multimodal prompts — whether through text, visual cues, or marking time segments.

item.title: How Conservation X Labs Is Using Segment Anything Model 3 for Endangered Wildlife Monitoring
item.description: Start-ups like Conservation X Labs are using Meta’s Segment Anything Models to bolster on-the-ground conservation expertise.

item.title: ExecuTorch Adoption in Reality Labs: Powering On-Device AI Across Meta Devices
item.description: ExecuTorch, Meta’s open source, lightweight, and efficient inference engine, has been instrumental in enabling on-device AI capabilities across Meta’s family of apps.

item.title: Introducing SAM 3D: Powerful 3D Reconstruction for Physical World Images
item.description: This release introduces two new state-of-the-art models: SAM 3D Objects for object and scene reconstruction, and SAM 3D Body for human body and shape estimation.

item.title: Omnilingual ASR: Advancing Automatic Speech Recognition for 1,600+ Languages
item.description: We’re introducing Meta Omnilingual Automatic Speech Recognition, a suite of models providing automatic speech recognition capabilities for over 1,600 languages.

item.title: Agents Rule of Two: A Practical Approach to AI Agent Security
The Building Blocks of Agentic AI: From Kernels to Clusters
item.description: At PyTorch Conference 2025 in San Francisco, we unveiled five new projects spanning kernel languages, distributed systems, reinforcement learning, agentic frameworks, and edge AI deployment.

item.title: How Llama and Oracle are helping Instituto PROA kickstart careers for students in Brazil
item.description: Instituto PROA, a nonprofit organization in Brazil, has transformed its job preparation process for young candidates by leveraging Llama and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

item.title: How DINOv3 is helping World Resources Institute restore forests and farms globally
item.description: WRI and the Bezos Earth Fund used DINOv3 to develop an algorithm to accurately count individual trees from drone and satellite imagery.

item.title: Small robots, mighty vision: NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory's DINOv2-enabled robot rovers and the future of planetary exploration
item.description: Using DINOv2, the team at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory built a convenient robot operating system interface for robotic tasks.

item.title: DINOv3: Self-supervised learning for vision at unprecedented scale
item.description: DINOv3 scales self-supervised learning for images to create universal vision backbones that achieve absolute state-of-the-art performance across diverse domains, including web and satellite imagery.

item.title: How Llama helps Biofy Technologies in the fight against antibiotic resistance
item.description: Brazil, the biotech company Biofy Technologies has developed a groundbreaking platform using Llama that reduces diagnostic time for antibiotic resistance from five days to less than four hours.

item.title: How Upwork helps freelancers win more work with Llama
item.description: Upwork, one of the world’s largest work marketplaces, is using Llama to power Uma, its mindful AI, to help freelancers land jobs faster and more confidently.

item.title: Joining forces with AWS on a new program to help startups build with Llama
item.description: We're joining forces with Amazon Web Services to announce a new program that will provide resources and support to 30 promising startups in the U.S. that are building with Llama.

item.title: Modeling natural conversational dynamics with Seamless Interaction
How Llama helps drive engineering efficiency at a major Australian bank
item.description: ANZ, one of Australia's Big Four banks, is driving engineering efficiency with Llama.

item.title: Announcing the inaugural Llama Startup Program cohort
item.description: At Meta, we believe in the potential of early-stage startups to drive innovation in the generative AI market, and through the Llama Startup Program, we aim to lower the barrier to entry for getting started with Llama models.

item.title: Introducing the V-JEPA 2 world model and new benchmarks for physical reasoning
item.description: We’re excited to share V-JEPA 2, the first world model trained on video that enables state-of-the-art understanding and prediction, as well as zero-shot planning and robot control in new environments.

item.title: Inside Aria Gen 2: Explore the cutting-edge tech behind the device
item.description: Today, we’re excited to share more about the technology inside Aria Gen 2. This includes an in-depth overview of the form factor, audio capabilities, battery life, upgraded cameras and sensors, on-device compute, and more.

item.title: Introducing the Llama Startup Program
item.description: We’re excited to announce the Llama Startup Program, a new initiative to empower early stage startups to innovate and build generative AI applications with Llama.

item.title: Sharing new breakthroughs and artifacts supporting molecular property prediction, language processing, and neuroscience
item.description: Meta FAIR is sharing new research artifacts that highlight our commitment to advanced machine intelligence (AMI) through focused scientific and academic progress.

item.title: Meet the winners of our first-ever LlamaCon Hackathon
item.description: The challenge was to create a demonstrable project using the Llama API, Llama 4 Scout, or Llama 4 Maverick—or any combination of these cutting-edge tools—within just 24 hours.

item.title: How Meta Segment Anything Model enables Cutouts in the Instagram Edits app
item.description: People can use Cutouts to edit across several layers of video, applying filters to specific parts of videos and easily place elements like text and stickers behind objects.

item.title: How Common Sense Machines uses Meta Segment Anything Model and AI to generate production-ready 3D assets
item.description: Common Sense Machines uses Meta Segment Anything Model 2 to analyze 2D images and video and translate their components to 3D.

item.title: Everything we announced at our first-ever LlamaCon
item.description: Here’s a look at what we announced at LlamaCon and how you can get started with our newest releases.

item.title: Sharing new open source protection tools and advancements in AI privacy and security
item.description: Today, we’re releasing new Llama protection tools for the open source AI community.

item.title: How Litmos is using Llama to make corporate learning more intuitive
item.description: With Llama 3.1 8B, Litmos created an AI-powered assistant that enables natural language search, making the LMS more intuitive and user-friendly.

item.title: Advancing AI systems through progress in perception, localization, and reasoning
item.description: Meta FAIR is releasing several new research artifacts that advance our understanding of perception and support our goal of achieving advanced machine intelligence (AMI).

item.title: The Llama 4 herd: The beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI innovation
item.description: We’re introducing Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, the first open-weight natively multimodal models with unprecedented context support and our first built using a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture.

item.title: How Tavus is helping to make AI videos feel like real conversations
item.description: Tavus, an AI video research company, leverages advanced AI models to create human-like digital interactions that feel as authentic as real human conversations.

item.title: How Cornerstone is transforming XR training to build essential skills for the modern workforce
item.description: Cornerstone is transforming how employees learn. Using Llama, they’re taking that experience to the next level through extended reality training scenarios.

item.title: Meta at SXSW: How Llama and open source AI is leveling the playing field and enabling innovation
item.description: At SXSW, Meta and Llama developers showcased the impact of open source AI, and participated in policy discussions and hands-on demos.

item.title: Our open source Llama models are helping to spur economic growth in the US
item.description: We’ve shared how Llama’s impact has already been felt by businesses and entrepreneurs. And today, we’re looking at how Llama is helping to spur economic growth in the US.

item.title: How Smartly is improving customer service and automating technical support with generative AI
item.description: Smartly, a digital advertising platform, harnesses the power of AI to scale ads across channels.

item.title: Building foundation models for understanding human pathology using DINOv2
item.description: When Dr. Faisal Mahmood set up his laboratory at the intersection of pathology and machine learning. The team credits open source models, including Meta’s DINO, with helping them do their work.

item.title: How Sofya is taking clinical reasoning to the next level with Llama
item.description: Sofya turned to Llama with the goal of fostering the development of healthcare solutions in Brazil and Latin America.

item.title: How Sevilla FC is discovering future soccer stars with Llama
item.description: Sevilla FC’s data department partnered with IBM to create Scout Advisor, a generative AI-driven scouting tool designed and deployed on watsonx, with Llama 3.1 70B Instruct.

item.title: How Orakl Oncology is using DINOv2 to accelerate cancer treatment discovery
item.description: Orakl Oncology aims to accelerate cancer research and drug development by combining experimental, lab-based insights and machine learning.

item.title: EgoMimic: Georgia Tech PhD student uses Project Aria Research Glasses to help train humanoid robots
item.description: Today, we’re highlighting new research from Georgia Tech that helps train robots to perform basic everyday tasks using egocentric recordings from wearers of Meta’s Project Aria research glasses.

item.title: Dramatically accelerating patient-matching in clinical trials with open source
item.description: Mendel AI’s flagship product, Mendel Hypercube, tackles clinical tasks. With Llama, Hypercube can also be used for trial matching and putting together patient groups.

item.title: AI Artifacts: An interview with Ruben Fro and Benjamin Bardou
item.description: In honor of this year’s AI Action Summit, we partnered with the Bibliothèque Nationale de France (BnF), Fisheye Immersive, Convergence, and Institut Montaigne to commission two open source AI-generated art installations by innovative artists, Ruben Fro and Benjamin Bardou.

item.title: Update: Expanding access to Meta Segment Anything 2.1 on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
item.description: Starting today, SAM 2.1 is available in Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, making it easier than ever to deploy SAM 2.1 and integrate it into new applications and workflows.

item.title: Reimagining K-12 Education: How Blended Labs is raising the bar with AI-driven schools
item.description: Blended, an educational technology and AI company based in Germany, is using Llama 3.1 as the foundation of its flagship product, Blended OS, an AI-native school operating system.

item.title: Using AI to decode language from the brain and advance our understanding of human communication
item.description: Today, in collaboration with the Basque Center on Cognition, Brain and Language, we’re excited to share two breakthroughs that show how AI can help advance our understanding of human intelligence, leading us closer to AMI.

item.title: Advancing machine intelligence through human-centered research
item.description: Meta FAIR is sharing some of our most recent research and models that support our goal of achieving AMI, and our long-held commitment to sharing open and reproducible science.

item.title: Enlisting Llama in India’s first open source audio language model
item.description: Sarvam used Llama to develop enterprise voice AI agents with improved reasoning capabilities that are proficient in 10 Indian languages.

item.title: How Altana employs Llama to elevate global value chain management
item.description: Altana uses AI to help businesses and governments manage their global supply chains, providing insight into extended supplier and distribution networks.

item.title: How BrightHeart uses Meta’s DINOv2 to transform fetal heart screenings
item.description: BrightHeart has created an AI-powered medical software with the support of Meta’s DINOv2 model to help clinicians identify or rule out signs suggestive of congenital heart defects.

item.title: Scaling an AI coding assistant built with Llama to hundreds of thousands of users
item.description: Codeium’s AI-enabled coding assistant leverages Llama for its free and premiere offerings.

item.title: How digital artist Josephine Miller uses Meta Segment Anything to help design the future of fashion
item.description: Josephine, a London-based XR creative designer and content creator, takes us behind the scenes of how she uses open source technology in her work.

item.title: How Llama is helping Saama deliver new possibilities in personalized medicine and data-driven care
item.description: OpenBioLLM, a series of fine-tuned Llama models from life sciences company Saama, streamlines tasks that can accelerate clinical trials and potentially create new possibilities in personalized medicine.

item.title: How Virgo is using DINOv2 to analyze endoscopy videos for precision medicine
item.description: Virgo, a company based in San Diego, California, believes endoscopy video and AI are a powerful combination that could lead to improved patient outcomes.

item.title: How TU Dresden is advancing precision oncology and transforming healthcare AI
item.description: TUD Dresden University of Technology’s Clinical AI research group is transforming cancer care through computational innovation, focusing on precision oncology.

item.title: Introducing Nymeria, a dataset for improving human motion prediction for AR and VR devices
item.description: The Nymeria dataset provides egocentric human motion in the wild at an unprecedented scale, capturing a broad spectrum of people engaging in everyday activities across varied locations.

item.title: Finding the perfect book to read next with a Llama-based assistant
item.description: Scribd, Inc.’s Everand reading service is home to a global library of millions of ebooks, audiobooks and more.

item.title: The future of AI: Built with Llama
item.description: As we close out 2024, Meta is leading the industry forward in AI product and technology experiences and setting a new standard for how the industry builds and advances AI.

item.title: How Spotify is using Llama to create personalized recommendations and enhance content discovery
item.description: Spotify uses Llama to deliver contextualized recommendations to boost artist discovery and create an even richer user experience.

item.title: Sharing new research, models, and datasets from Meta FAIR
item.description: Meta FAIR is releasing new research artifacts that highlight our recent innovations in developing agents, robustness and safety, and architectures that facilitate machine learning.

item.title: How Inarix is using DINOv2 to revolutionize the agricultural supply chain
item.description: Inarix is transforming the agricultural industry by turning smartphones into pocket laboratories using Meta FAIR’s DinoV2.

item.title: Advancing Neuromotor Interfaces by Open Sourcing Surface Electromyography (sEMG) Datasets for Pose Estimation and Surface Typing
item.description: We’re releasing emg2qwerty and emg2pose—two large datasets and benchmarks for sEMG-based typing and pose estimation, as part of the NeurIPS 2024 Datasets and Benchmarks track.

item.title: Scaling semiconductor expertise with Llama-powered Domain-Expert Agents
item.description: Aitomatic enables semiconductor companies to build Domain-Expert Agents to capture and scale their deep domain expertise using Llama-based foundation models.

item.title: Extending healthcare access to underserved communities with AI
item.description: The team behind BiMediX2, an Arabic-English medical large multimodal model, aims to expand healthcare access in Africa and the Middle East.

item.title: Introducing SPDL: Faster AI model training with thread-based data loading
item.description: SPDL is a framework-agnostic data loading solution that uses multi-threading, which achieves high-throughput in a regular Python interpreter (built without free-threading option enabled).

item.title: Building a chatbot based on Llama to engage an intergovernmental organization’s stakeholders
item.description: Accenture turned to Llama for a leading global intergovernmental body’s first large-scale, public-facing generative AI application.

item.title: Open Catalyst experiments 2024
item.description: Meta FAIR is releasing a large-scale dataset of experimental results on various materials, providing valuable insights for the development of new catalysts.

item.title: IBM and Llama: Working to enable AI builder creativity globally
item.description: IBM and Meta are implementing the combined power of IBM’s watsonx AI and data platform and Llama to help businesses reach their AI goals.

item.title: Highlights from the first-ever Llama hackathon in India
item.description: Teams embarked on a 30-hour journey to create AI solutions tackling real-world challenges, leveraging Meta’s Llama 3 models and WhatsApp APIs.

item.title: Advancing embodied AI through progress in touch perception, dexterity, and human-robot interaction
item.description: Meta FAIR is publicly releasing several new research artifacts that advance robotics and support our goal of reaching advanced machine intelligence (AMI).

item.title: Roboflow estimates a 74-year time savings across its community from using Meta’s Segment Anything
item.description: As a company with a mission to help make the world more programmable, Roboflow uses SAM to help build systems that enable their customers to have visual understanding for just about anything.

item.title: Introducing quantized Llama models with increased speed and a reduced memory footprint
item.description: As our first quantized models in this Llama category, these instruction-tuned models retain the quality and safety of the original 1B and 3B models, while achieving 2-4x speedup.

item.title: How an online gifting site is using Llama to help protect customer privacy
item.description: Untukmu.AI, a platform that offers personalized gift recommendations, designed a semi-decentralized personal assistant that ensures the company won’t have access to customer data.

item.title: Sharing new research, models, and datasets from Meta FAIR
item.description: Today, Meta FAIR is releasing several new research artifacts in support of our goal of achieving advanced machine intelligence (AMI) while also supporting open science and reproducibility.

item.title: Partnering with Blumhouse, creators, and the entertainment industry as we develop Meta Movie Gen
item.description: We’re sharing initial results from our work with award-winning production company Blumhouse and select creators—part of a pilot program focused on creative industry feedback.

item.title: How Llama helped CodeGPT become one of the top AI-powered coding assistants
item.description: CodeGPT integrates large language models like Llama to make developers and CTOs more productive.

item.title: How Neuromnia is transforming ABA therapy with Llama 3.1
item.description: Harnessing Llama 3.1, Neuromnia recently developed Nia, a human-centric AI co-pilot for Applied Behavior Analysis therapy.

item.title: How Meta Movie Gen could usher in a new AI-enabled era for content creators
item.description: Today, we’re excited to premiere Meta Movie Gen, our breakthrough generative AI research for media, which includes modalities like image, video, and audio.
